name: Re-Engagement / Win-Back Broadcast — Cold Subscriber Reactivation

sections: DotCom Secrets Secret #6 — Us vs Them & Secret Telling storylines, power of polarity
DotCom Secrets Secret #8 — daily emailing keeps the relationship alive ('if I don't email my list every day, I lose money')
Copywriting Secrets — Takeaway/FOMO close, '9 of 10 buy out of dissatisfaction with current circumstances'

trigger.primary: A subscriber on the broadcast segment goes COLD — no email open or click in 30 days (then escalates at 60). Protects sender reputation and revives lapsed buyers.
trigger.trigger_type_ghl: Workflow on email-engagement: when last_email_open is older than 30 days -> Add Tag 'cold-30d' (implemented via a recurring/scheduled workflow checking the last_email_open custom field, or GHL email-event filters).

channels: SMS (one nudge on the final takeaway)

sequence.purpose: Re-open the loop; force identity recommitment.
sequence.subjectAngle: Curiosity + Us vs Them line-in-the-sand: 'Still a do-er... or a talker?'
sequence.bodyFramework: Short, personal, polarizing. Secret-telling hook to spark a re-open. Single CTA = click to stay.
sequence.branch: Goal: any open/click -> 're-engaged' -> remove cold tag, exit to active broadcast.

sequence.purpose: Remind them why they joined; deliver dissatisfaction-relief.
sequence.subjectAngle: 'The one story everyone replied to' / 'your invite is still open'.
sequence.bodyFramework: Lead with your single most entertaining Seinfeld story + your strongest offer. Future-pace the result they wanted when they joined.
sequence.branch: Goal re-open -> exit to active broadcast.

sequence.purpose: Force a decision; clean the list if no response.
sequence.bodyFramework: Honest takeaway selling: 'I'll take you off unless you click below.' One CTA. + SMS nudge.
sequence.branch: No open/click after this -> tag 'sunset' + remove from broadcast Smart List.

copyPersona.primary: Brunson polarity / Us vs Them ('talkers vs do-ers') + Secret Telling storylines (DCS Secret #6).
copyPersona.secondary: Edwards Takeaway/FOMO close + 'people buy out of dissatisfaction' — frame the win-back as relief from their unchanged situation.

ghlBuild.buildType: A real GoHighLevel WORKFLOW (sequenced win-back drip with a goal exit and a sunset suppression).
ghlBuild.trigger: No email open in 30 days -> Add Tag 'cold-30d' (scheduled workflow checking last_email_open custom field, or GHL engagement filter). Workflow start = Tag Added 'cold-30d'.
actions: Goal: Email Opened OR Link Clicked -> branch 're-engaged' (Remove Tag cold-30d, keep seinfeld-broadcast, exit)
If still no open/click -> Add Tag 'sunset' + Remove Tag 'seinfeld-broadcast' (drops them from the daily Smart List) + Remove from broadcast
ghlBuild.goalEvent: Trigger-link / Goal on 'Email Opened' or 'Link Clicked' re-routes to the re-engaged branch and removes the cold tag at any step.
ghlBuild.notes: Removing 'seinfeld-broadcast' from non-responders protects daily-send deliverability. Set action order + parentKey before first save (Skill 44).

kpis: deliverability lift after sunset (bounce/spam rate drop)
faithfulness: Copy ANGLES (Us vs Them 'talkers vs do-ers', Secret Telling, takeaway selling, 'buy out of dissatisfaction') are taken from DotCom Secrets Secret #6 and Copywriting Secrets. The TRIGGER (no-open-30d) and the sunset suppression are standard email-deliverability practice DERIVED from the books' 'email daily or lose money' + polarity/takeaway principles — flagged as derived, NOT a verbatim Brunson sequence.
